# `btpc completion install`

<!-- Generated by `scripts/generate-cli-reference.sh`; do not edit. -->

Install shell completions in the standard per-user directory

**Parent:** [`btpc completion`](completion.md)

See [Configuration and global flags](../configuration.md) and [Exit codes and streams](../index.md#exit-codes-and-streams).

## Synopsis

```text
Usage: btpc completion install [OPTIONS] [SHELL]
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `<SHELL>` | Shell to install; detected from environment hints when omitted Values: `bash`, `elvish`, `fish`, `powershell`, `zsh`. |

## Options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--dry-run` | Print the target and generated content without changing files Default: `false`. |
| `--force` | Replace an unrelated existing completion file Default: `false`. |
| `-h, --help` | Print help |

## Global options

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--config, <PATH>` | Use this configuration file when configuration loading is enabled |
| `--no-config` | Disable implicit and environment-selected configuration Default: `false`. |
| `--color, <COLOR>` | Control colored terminal output Values: `auto`, `always`, `never`. |
| `-v, --verbose` | Increase diagnostic verbosity; may be repeated Default: `0`. |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress human summaries, warnings, and progress Default: `false`. |

## Example

```console
$ btpc completion install --help
```
